_comment: Cross-language byte-equality fixtures for domain separators. v2 hardening (2026-05-15, post Security audit C-1): the separator is now a 32-byte keccak256 of the typed-domain label, NOT the raw UTF-8 bytes. Pre-v2 form retained under `_v1_legacy` for reference only. If you add a new separator, regenerate via `npm run generate-domain-separator-fixtures` (TS side) and re-run Python parity test. Both sides assert byte-equality against this file. Drift here means cross-language signatures will not interoperate.

DOMAIN_SEPARATOR_SIMULATE_v1_raw_string.note: DEPRECATED. Pre-v2 weak structural separation. Do not use.

DOMAIN_SEPARATOR_SIMULATE.label: PaladinFi/simulate/v2
DOMAIN_SEPARATOR_SIMULATE.note: 32-byte keccak256 of label. Used as OUTER hash prefix: digest = keccak256(this || keccak256(JCS(body)))
